task: Fix Cloudflare Pages preview routing so expanded Test Project 1 journal seed data appears on PR previews.
evidence: The reported preview URL redirected unauthenticated local requests to Cloudflare Access.
public/_worker.js already routes preview-host /api/* requests to the passwordless preview API.
public/js/journal-tabs.js and public/js/caqdas-interface.js hard-coded the production API worker for pages.dev hosts.
infra/cloudflare/src/service/journals.js returned any D1 rows before checking whether Test Project 1 D1 data was partial.
changes: Journal and CAQDAS scripts now use same-origin /api/* on pages.dev unless an explicit API origin is configured.
Journal D1 project lookup now aliases Test Project 1 canonical id, legacy id and local id.
Partial Test Project 1 D1 journal populations now fall back to and restore the complete expanded seed.
Route-state and runtime tests cover preview-safe API routing and partial D1 seed recovery.
validationNotRun: Authenticated preview browser verification was not possible because the preview is Cloudflare Access protected from this environment.
residualRisks: The live Pages preview requires Cloudflare deployment completion before user verification.
A preview API environment with stale non-journal seed tables could still need the existing code/memo/co-occurrence fallbacks until remote D1 migration catches up.
